data.body:
GATE VALIDATION (final_gate_3pass.py, v8 rubric, agentic Pass-2) on the realigned epic. RESULT: DET floor PASS (check-ac 6; clarity 7/5); 31 Pass-1 findings -> 0 BLOCK, 29 advisory, 2 dropped. Zero blocking (v1 posture).

The advisory count is high (29 vs ~10-13 on the mature plan-review epic) because b744 is a LEAN, UNDECOMPOSED capability epic (5.2k chars, 0 children) — the gate is flagging pervasive UNDER-SPECIFICATION (G5 'decompose'; T8 Gerrit LLM-Review integer-label vocabulary undefined; the T2/T4/T5x/T6/T7/T9/T12 overlay details). That is the expected 'this capability epic needs decomposition + detailing' backlog, NOT a defect in the prerequisite realignment, and remediating it = designing/decomposing the whole epic (a separate effort from 'reflect recent codebase changes').

FOUR findings that directly REFINED the realignment edits were fixed:
- E3/E6: AC1 source-separation must PRESERVE the existing review_code MCP tool + 'rebar review-code' CLI callers (re-export/shim).
- E1: AC2 three-pass migration must also RETIRE the single-pass evidence+aggregate path (no half-migration).
- T5b/COH (critical): AC5 — the hard secrets/security auto-block must NOT inherit the oracle's fail-open posture; on detector-input unavailability it FAILS CLOSED (or surfaces a coverage gap on the vote), so a high-precision block is never silently skipped.
- E1: AC3 reframed — the shipped PydanticAIRunner + review_code are PRECONDITIONS; the deliverable is wiring the capability onto them (the prior phrasing verified no new deliverable).

Re-verified: check-ac + clarity still pass. The remaining advisory backlog (decomposition + overlay/Gerrit-label detail) is the natural next step for this capability epic — flagged for a decomposition pass when the user is ready.
